other_observers: Cole Wild Norm Erthal Duane Nelson Stan Oswald Nick Komar; As I understand it the bird was first spotted by Norm, Cole, and Nick, but was originally identified as a Ruff until Duane Nelson identified the bird correctly.
specific_location: about one quarter mile inland from the outlet to Adobe Creek Reservoir State Wildlife Area, a.k.a know as Blue Lake
description: Mostly gray medium-sized shorebird smaller than Black-bellied Plovers with which it occasionally associated and much larger than Baird's Sandpiper which were seen closer to the road. Bill blackish and of medium length, longer i.e than the Black-bellied Plovers' bills. Very long wings and shortish green gray legs. Whitish supercillium. Breast with faint pinkish tinge (which is found on juveniles, but not always seen, and is different than the red of an adult). Faint medium gray eyestripe just below the supercillium. Wing coverts and scapulars strongly edged with white with a dark subterminal dark band and gray feather centers. A faint dark feather shaft visible when bird was the closest, but not when far away. In flight a white wing stipe was visible.Behaviors: First seen on shoreline by itself whre it probed in the mud. Later joined up with four Black-bellied Plovers and together foragged the shoreline before flying off together.Call: none heardPlumage: junvenile bird with some juvenal plumage
